title: notifications-in-app — background jobs
description: One job. Thin because in-app delivery is a synchronous DB write + a fire-and-forget broadcast.

jobs.purpose: Consumes NotificationDispatched (channel=in_app). Creates the NotificationDelivery row via core's DispatchGateway with state=delivered. Emits `notifications.created` broadcast on user.{id}.notifications. Broadcast failure is logged but does NOT retry — the DB write is the ground truth, socket updates are best-effort.
steps: Load Notification by id from event payload.
On broadcast failure: log NOTIFICATIONS_INAPP_BROADCAST_FAILED, emit metric increment, no retry.
jobs.notes: uniqueFor 60s prevents duplicate broadcast if a worker restarts mid-job. tries=2 covers DB flakes; broadcast failures are non-retryable.
